item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 28, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Small piece of crockery
  • Hint 2: Used for cups or mugs
  • Hint 3: Flying ______

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Mug
  • Cup
  • Porcelain
  • Vase
  • Bowl
  • Spoon
  • Cream
  • Teapot
  • Teacup

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 28, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Saucer

A Saucer is a small, curved plate that you put a cup on.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 29.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Senzu Joins BC.Game on Loan From Passion UA
item.content:encoded:
Just one day after placing third at Frag 20 with Passion UA, Azbayar ‘Senzu’ Munkhbold has joined BC.Game on loan.

The Mongolian Counter-Strike pro will play alongside Oleksandr ‘s1mple’ Kostyliev and BC.Game’s assistant coach, Robin ‘ScrunK⁠’ Röpke.

The coach is filling in for the team’s next two tournaments, IEM Atlanta and the CS Asia Championship, with the team looking for a new permanent IGL after the Major.

This comes after BC.Game benched Christopher “‘MUTiRiS⁠’ Fernandes and António “‘aragornN⁠’ Barbosa earlier this month.

BC.Game has struggled for results recently, following a Group Stage exit at PGL Bucharest and failing to qualify for the Cologne Major.

Meanwhile, Senzu only spent just over two months with Passion UA, having joined from MongolZ back in February.

Passion UA have struggled for form, though Senzu has put in decent performances with a team-high average 1.21 rating.

However, Senzu missed out on IEM Rio as tournament rules required his team to field more than one North American player.

For the next two events, BC.Game’s roster will be:

  • Oleksandr “‘s1mple⁠’ Kostyliev
  • Russia Denis “‘electroNic⁠’ Sharipov
  • Portugal Adones “‘krazy⁠’ Nobre
  • Mongolia Azbayar ‘⁠Senzu⁠’ Munkhbold
  • Germany Robin ‘ScrunK⁠’ Röpke
  • Poland Wiktor “⁠TaZ⁠” Wojtas (coach)

“What I like about Senzu is the positions he will be taking will also enable us to give electroNic more space to shine on the positions he used to play back in the day on NAVI,” says BC.Game coach Wiktor ‘TaZ’ Wojtas.

Meanwhile, Passion UA will now field Viktor ‘sdy’ Orudzhev in Senzu’s place.

Sdy is another former ENCE player who has had to find a new role, just like FaZe’s new stand-in signing Ryan ‘Neityu⁠’ Aubry.

Do you think this is a good move for Senzu and BC.Game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And outside of BC.Game’s next events, there’s also the BLAST Rivals Fort Worth tournament to look forward to.

MOBILE APP
